confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Passages advanced to evidence-review. Score of 3 reflects partial corrigibility: present in the War Powers Resolution and Carter-era crisis acknowledgment, but contested and partially reversed in the Reagan era. Confidence raised from 0.58. Capped pending chain expansion.

confidence.label: moderate
confidence.rationale: Score of 3 (moderate) for the 1973–1984 post-Vietnam period: the symbolic order retained democratic institutional coherence but lost Cold War consensus, executive war authority, and public confidence (per Carter). Mid-range score reflects the order's ambiguous status — neither collapsed (Soviet 1991) nor highly constrained (postwar Germany) nor strongly expansive (imperial Japan). Confidence at 0.60 because periodization is genuinely contested: Reagan-era rearmament suggests symbolic-order strength recovered more than a 3 implies by the mid-1980s.
